name: Route of the Ponds
name_original: Ruta de las Tres Charcas — circular por el Monumento Natural de Los Barruecos
description: Jog the 5.2-mile Route of the Three Ponds in Los Barruecos, a moderate trail through granite formations and past white stork colonies.
region_intro: Jogging in Monumento Natural Los Barruecos offers routes through a distinctive landscape characterized by peculiar granitic formations known as "barruecos." The terrain is generally a flat peneplain, making it accessible for various fitness levels. Interspersed within this granite landscape are several artificial lakes and ponds, which reflect the rock formations and provide habitats for diverse wildlife. The area features varied flora, including oaks, cork oaks, and willows near wetlands, creating a natural backdrop for running. Best jogging routes in Monumento Natural Los Barruecos The most popular jogging route is Route of the Ponds, a 5.2 miles (8.4 km) trail that takes about 1 hour to complete. This moderate route leads through the distinctive granite landscape and past the artificial lakes. Another top favourite among local runners is El Bolo, Los Barruecos – Los Barruecos Natural Monument loop from Monumento Natural Los Barruecos, a moderate 4.2 miles (6.8 km) path. This route offers views of the unique rock formations and opportunities for wildlife spotting. Local runners also love the Lower Barrueco Pond – Los Barruecos Natural Monument loop from Monumento Natural Los Barruecos, a 4.1 miles (6.6 km) trail leading through the flat peneplain and past the serene ponds, often completed in about 44 minutes. Jogging in Monumento Natural Los Barruecos is defined by its unique granitic formations, artificial lakes, and diverse flora. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy paths to moderate routes. The routes in Monumento Natural Los Barruecos are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.5 stars from over 6 reviews. More than 500 runners have used komoot to explore Monumento Natural Los Barruecos's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes can I find in Monumento Natural Los Barruecos?
A: Monumento Natural Los Barruecos offers a good selection of routes for runners, with &lt;strong&gt;over 15 different trails&lt;/strong&gt; available. These routes vary in length and difficulty, catering to a range of fitness levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when jogging in Monumento Natural Los Barruecos?
A: The terrain in Monumento Natural Los Barruecos is generally a &lt;strong&gt;flat peneplain&lt;/strong&gt;, making it quite accessible for jogging. You'll encounter peculiar granitic formations, known as 'barruecos,' scattered across the landscape, along with several artificial lakes and ponds. The paths are typically not overly challenging in terms of elevation.

Q: Are there any easy running routes suitable for beginners or a relaxed jog?
A: Yes, there are several easy running options. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990937456/las-charcas-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Charcas Trail&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 2.5-mile (4 km) path. Another great choice is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31879329" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Bolo, Los Barruecos – Lower Barrueco Pond loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 2.6 miles (4.2 km) and also considered easy.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks I might see along the running trails?
A: You'll be surrounded by unique natural features. The most striking are the &lt;strong&gt;granitic rock formations&lt;/strong&gt; ('barruecos') and the serene artificial lakes. Keep an eye out for the large colony of &lt;strong&gt;white storks&lt;/strong&gt; nesting atop the granite rocks. You can also visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4396799" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lower Barrueco Pond&lt;/a&gt; or explore the area around &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4615460" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Bolo, Los Barruecos&lt;/a&gt;, both popular spots.

Q: Can I spot any wildlife while running in Monumento Natural Los Barruecos?
A: Absolutely! Monumento Natural Los Barruecos is a haven for wildlife, especially birds. Besides the famous white storks, you might see ducks, herons, and various raptors like ornate hawk eagles. The ponds also support other animals such as otters and newts, making for an engaging natural experience.

Q: Are there any cultural or historical attractions near the running paths?
A: Yes, the area is rich in history and culture. You can find archaeological sites dating back to the Paleolithic era, including remains of an old Roman villa and anthropomorphic tombs like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4296275" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Anthropomorphic Tomb&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;strong&gt;Vostell Malpartida Museum&lt;/strong&gt;, an 18th-century woolen washing place housing contemporary art, is also located within the park and integrates art with nature.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Monumento Natural Los Barruecos?
A: Many of the running routes in Monumento Natural Los Barruecos are designed as loops, offering a convenient way to explore the area. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31876983" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Bolo, Los Barruecos – Los Barruecos Natural Monument loop&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 4.2-mile (6.8 km) path that provides excellent views of the unique rock formations.

Q: What do other runners think about the trails in Monumento Natural Los Barruecos?
A: The running routes in Monumento Natural Los Barruecos are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average score of &lt;strong&gt;3.5 stars&lt;/strong&gt; from over 6 reviews. More than 500 runners have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the unique granite landscapes and the peaceful atmosphere around the ponds.

Q: Is Monumento Natural Los Barruecos suitable for family-friendly running or walks?
A: Yes, the generally flat terrain and varied route lengths make it suitable for families. The 'Ruta de los Sentidos' is a 1.5-kilometer accessible route, highlighting the park's inclusivity. Many paths are easy and offer opportunities to enjoy nature, spot wildlife, and see interesting rock formations together.

Q: Can I bring my dog along on the running trails?
A: While the region research doesn't explicitly state dog-friendly policies, natural parks in Spain often have specific rules regarding pets. It's always best to check local regulations or signage at the park entrance to ensure your dog is welcome on the trails and if they need to be on a leash.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Monumento Natural Los Barruecos?
A: The region offers pleasant conditions for outdoor activities for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally provide milder temperatures, which are ideal for running. Summers can be hot, so early mornings or late evenings are preferable, while winters are typically mild, allowing for year-round enjoyment.

Q: Are there any specific routes that pass by the artificial lakes or ponds?
A: Many routes are designed to showcase the beautiful water features. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1347520363/route-of-the-ponds" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Ponds&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, a 5.2-mile (8.4 km) trail that specifically leads through the distinctive granite landscape and past the artificial lakes. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31867545" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lower Barrueco Pond loop&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you past serene ponds.
tips: This is the largest Los Barruecos pond and the one located furthest south. In its area we can find curious granite rock formations and also in its surroundings you can visit the Vostell Malpartida museum.
This pond is the least known of the three and also the smallest in size, but in its surroundings we can also enjoy good views and good geological formations.

This wonderful place, declared a Natural Monument, is not only appreciated for its diversity of flora and fauna, but has also provided a natural setting for one of the scenes in the multi-award-winning Game of Thrones series. A place that is undoubtedly worth visiting.

In addition to the curious granite forms of the Los Barruecos area, in some of them we can see rock engravings, also known as "cazoletas". These are incisions in the granitic stone made between the Chalcolithic and the Bronze Age.

Anthropomorphic burials are those that present a human-shaped plant, with just enough space for the placement of the deceased in a supine position and a narrowing to force the deceased's head to rest facing forwards.

faqs.question: How long does it take to jog the Route of the Three Ponds?
faqs.answer: The komoot data suggests that jogging this 8.38 km circular route typically takes around 1 hour. However, if you choose to explore the various points of interest or take a more leisurely pace, it could take longer.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this route?
faqs.answer: The trail primarily features natural and unpaved paths, along with some compacted earth tracks. You'll navigate through a landscape dotted with impressive granite formations, known as 'bolos graníticos,' and pathways that traverse lagoons and streams. While generally accessible, some areas may require sure-footedness, and sections could be boggy during periods of high water. The overall landscape is undulating, adding slight variations to your run.

faqs.question: What unique natural and historical features can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: This route offers a rich blend of natural beauty and historical significance. You'll encounter the three main ponds: the Charca del Barrueco de Abajo, the Charca de Frasco Díez, and the Charca de Los Barruecos. Keep an eye out for the striking Rock Formations, which are a hallmark of the area. You might also discover an Anthropomorphic Tomb carved into the granite, showcasing the area's ancient heritage. The Vostell Malpartida Museum, housed in a former wool washery, is also located within the Natural Monument.

faqs.question: What wildlife can I expect to see?
faqs.answer: The Natural Monument of Los Barruecos is a haven for birdlife. It's particularly famous for its large colonies of white storks, which often nest atop the granite rocks. You might also spot other aquatic birds such as cormorants, grebes, and black-winged stilts, especially around the ponds.

faqs.question: Is the Route of the Three Ponds suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: The route is classified as 'moderate' for jogging, meaning a good level of fitness is generally recommended. While some similar routes in the area are considered easy for hiking, the specific demands of jogging this 8.38 km trail with its undulating terrain and natural paths make it more suitable for those with some jogging experience.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this route?
faqs.answer: The Natural Monument of Los Barruecos is beautiful year-round, but spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions for jogging, with milder temperatures and vibrant landscapes. During these seasons, you can also observe the active birdlife, especially the white storks. Summers can be very hot, and some sections might be boggy after heavy winter rains.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trail?
faqs.answer: Specific parking information for the Route of the Three Ponds isn't detailed, but the Natural Monument of Los Barruecos typically has designated parking areas for visitors. It's advisable to check local signage upon arrival for the most convenient access points to the trail.

faqs.question: Is the Natural Monument of Los Barruecos dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While dogs are generally welcome in many natural areas, specific rules regarding leashing and waste disposal within the Natural Monument of Los Barruecos can vary. It's always best to keep your dog on a leash to protect the local wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all visitors. Please clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for the Natural Monument of Los Barruecos?
faqs.answer: The Natural Monument of Los Barruecos is generally open to the public without an entrance fee. However, specific regulations or permits might apply for certain activities or larger groups. It's always a good idea to check the official website or local information points for any updates before your visit.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route shares sections with several other official trails within the Natural Monument. You'll find it intersects with parts of the Ruta de las Charcas, the Ruta Patrimonio Geológico y Arqueológico, and the Ruta del Barrueco de Arriba, among others. This offers opportunities to explore different facets of the monument if you wish to extend your run.
